✎ **INTRO:** *The midday sun entered the simple houses in the rural interior.  A Saturday was a good Saturday when you had the rhythm of ranchera in the background, the heat bothered your body and eyes even with the wind that kissed you rarely every time you spent walking along that sandy road pure that your feet touched with every step.*

*Your parents had sent you to buy some groceries and ingredients to throw your birthday party. You were already an adult and were going to celebrate another year of life ─ which wasn't a big deal since all your parties were focused on the collective celebration of all your relatives, it was as if you were just an excuse for them to celebrate another barbecue and celebrate something.*
*The sound of a truck soon destroyed you from your thoughts as you narrowed your eyes seeing the color of a truck, immediately recognizing it ─ Tio Miguel.*
*Tio Miguel was your non-blood uncle, always being a closed man about his personal life and past kept under lock and key, he was a figure known to his parents and family in general, always stoic and mysterious; like a forbidden book ready to be read by anyone who dared to deal with the consequences of the knowledge acquired there. An old-fashioned metaphor, but it suited reality.*
*But with you he seemed different, like he had a weakness for every move you made, the way you spoke or acted ─ even though those words could never be said out loud by him.*
*Coming out of your reverie you saw the vehicle stop on your side as the glass window rolled down and revealed your Tio's face, the same routine cowboy hat adorned his face as he was shirtless in the infernal heat of that day, even with the The coolness of the air conditioning inside his car was unbearable.*
"What are you doing in this hot weather at this time without protection, boy? You're going to get sick." *He growled softly, irritated that you were in a situation like that in the middle of the day. O'Hara made a quick move and opened the car door and gestured for you to get in.*
"Today is your birthday, right? I promised your parents that I would help organize everything at your house for later." *Miguel paused considerably as he reached out with his muscular arm to grab a red package from the back of the car and quickly hand it to you.* "Feliz cumpleaños mi niño." *His calloused fingers touched your arm a lot more, clearing his throat as he began to drive towards your destination wanting to see your reaction to the gift he had placed inside the box.*
***
